zh.lifeSummary: Isabell Denton是Thomas Denton和Jane Webbe的女兒，於1490年出生在英國奧可福爾。她於1530年與William Walter在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1588年1月27日在英國越可爾吉可波頓享年98歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Isabell Denton是Thomas Denton和Jane Webbe的女儿，于1490年出生在英国奧可福爾。她于1530年与William Walter在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1588年1月27日在英国越可爾吉可波頓享年98岁。

nameMeanings.name: Denton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places so called. The vast majority, including those in Cambridgeshire, Cumbria, Dumfries, Durham, Kent, Lancashire, Lincolnshire, Norfolk, Northumberland, Oxfordshire, Sussex, and Yorkshire are named from Old English denu ‘valley’ (see Dean 1) + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’. An isolated example in Northamptonshire appears in the Domesday Book as Dodintone meaning ‘enclosure, settlement associated with Dodda or Dudda’.

variants.name: Dean
meanings: English: topographic name from Middle English dene ‘valley’ (Old English denu), or a habitational name from any of several places in various parts of England named Dean or Dene from this word.

English: nickname or occupational name for the servant of a dean or nickname for someone thought to resemble a dean. A dean was an ecclesiastical official, the head of a chapter of canons or a church official with jurisdiction over a sub-division of an archdeaconry. Though no doubt some deans had illegitimate children, they were officially celibate, and in the main the surname is probably a nickname in origin, similar to Bishop , Prior , Priest , and Monk . The Middle English word deen, dien, dein, is a borrowing of Old French d(e)ien, doien from Latin decanus (originally a leader of ten men, from decem ‘ten’), and thus is a cognate of Deacon .
English: from the Middle English personal name Deyne (or Dene) a rhyming pet form of Reynald (see Reginald ).

nameMeanings.name: Isabell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: Isabell Denton was born in 1490, in Oxfordshire, England as the daughter of Thomas Denton and Jane Webbe. She married William Walter in 1530, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. She died on 27 January 1588, in Kirkburton, Yorkshire, England, at the age of 98.

fr.lifeSummary: Isabell Denton est née en 1490, à Oxfordshire, Angleterre, fille de Thomas Denton et Jane Webbe. Elle a épousé William Walter en 1530, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 27 janvier 1588, à Kirkburton, Everwyck, Angleterre, à l’âge de 98 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Isabell Denton wurde 1490 in Oxfordshire, England, als Tochter von Thomas Denton und Jane Webbe geboren. Sie hat 1530 in England, United Kingdom William Walter geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 27. Januar 1588 im Alter von 98 Jahren in Kirkburton, Yorkshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Isabell Denton è nata il nel 1490 nel Oxfordshire, England come figlia di Thomas Denton e Jane Webbe. Lei sposato William Walter nel 1530, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 3 figlie. Morì il 27 gennaio 1588, nel Kirkburton, Yorkshire, England, all'età di 98 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Isabell Denton(은)는 1490년 영국 스퍼드실르에서 Thomas Denton(와)과 Jane Webbe의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1530년 영국 잉글랜드에서 William Walter(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1588년 1월 27일 영국 요르크실르 커k버턴에서 98세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Isabell Denton родилась в 1490 г. в местности оксфордшир, Англия. Её родителей звали Thomas Denton и Jane Webbe. Она умерла 27 января 1588 г. в местности киркбуртон, еркшир, Англия в возрасте 98 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Isabell Denton nació en 1490, en Oxfordshire, Inglaterra como hija de Thomas Denton y Jane Webbe. Se casó con William Walter en 1530, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
